headline: Peyton Elizabeth Lee Revs Up Teen Comedy Machine with American High's Latest Romp
lede: Fresh off the success of her viral hit 'Pizza Movie', Peyton Elizabeth Lee is diving back into comedy with a high-concept project that promises to push genre boundaries.
body:
American High continues its winning streak by reuniting with rising star Peyton Elizabeth Lee for 'Second First Date', a bold R-rated comedy that signals the studio's aggressive expansion into more mature teen narratives.

Writer Matt Sacca, who demonstrated his comedic chops with 'Pizza Movie', will helm the screenplay. The project emerges from the production company's recent momentum, which saw their previous film dominate streaming charts on both Hulu and Disney+ following its breakout SXSW premiere.

The film represents a strategic pivot for American High, which has traditionally trafficked in lighter teen fare. By targeting an R-rated audience, the studio appears to be testing broader comedic territory while leveraging Lee's proven box office draw.

Industry watchers will be closely monitoring the project, given Lee's rapidly ascending profile and American High's recent track record of delivering unexpected hit comedies. The studio has consistently demonstrated an ability to blend commercial appeal with innovative storytelling.
key_facts: R-rated comedy targets mature teen audience
Project builds on American High's streaming success
